bindings.quest_action: restock the pantry using my husband's old supply routes
bindings.quest_focus: learning to manage the household alone after losing my husband
bindings.quest_stakes: the shelves are empty because I don't know how to do what he did
bindings.quest_threat: dangerous roads and unfamiliar supply routes
bindings.quest_origin: a fever that wouldn't break, three weeks ago

dialogueChunks.intro: Forgive me, {player_name}. I'm not usually like this. It's just... the house is so quiet now.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My husband passed three weeks ago. {quest_origin}. He was the one who managed our stores, kept everything full. Now {quest_stakes}. {target_npc} has been helping, but they have their own family. I need {quest_item} from the suppliers he used {location_hint}, but {quest_threat} and I don't know the routes.
dialogueChunks.outro: I'm not asking for pity. {quest_focus} is my burden, but I need help until I can {quest_action} on my own.

playerResponses.text: You don't need to stand alone yet. I'll get what you need.
Grief is hard enough without empty shelves. Tell me the route.
I'm sorry for your loss, but I can't take on every sad story.

bindings.quest_action: restore the garden my daughter grew from bare dirt
bindings.quest_focus: a garden that was my daughter's pride and life's work
bindings.quest_stakes: letting it die feels like losing her twice
bindings.quest_threat: the same enemies that took her still prowl the outskirts
bindings.quest_origin: a raid on the outskirts that took my daughter last month

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, do you see that garden over there? The one with the withered tomatoes? That was my daughter's.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: She was taken by {enemy_type} during {quest_origin}. {target_npc} tried to reach her but there were too many. Now {quest_focus} is dying because I can't bring myself to tend it. But {quest_stakes}. I need {quest_item} to {quest_action}: good seed, fresh soil. The supplier is {location_hint}, and {quest_threat} makes the roads unsafe.
dialogueChunks.outro: I know a garden won't bring her back. But she'd want it to thrive.

playerResponses.text: A garden kept alive is a memory kept alive. I'll get what you need.
She sounds like she was special. I'll honor her work.
I'm sorry. But a garden isn't something I risk my life for.

bindings.quest_action: complete the delivery my brother died trying to make
bindings.quest_focus: my brother's final journey to feed a desperate settlement
bindings.quest_stakes: the people he was trying to reach are still waiting and starving
bindings.quest_threat: the same dangerous road and enemies that killed him
bindings.quest_origin: an ambush on the road two months ago that took his life

dialogueChunks.intro: I should be cooking right now, {player_name}. That's what I do: I cook. But I can't seem to remember how anything tastes.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My brother was my taster, my critic, my loudest supporter. He died during {quest_origin} while delivering {quest_item} to a neighboring settlement. {target_npc} found his pack scattered {location_hint}. I want to {quest_action}. {quest_stakes}, and {quest_focus} deserves to be finished.
dialogueChunks.outro: He believed every meal could make someone's day better. {quest_threat} couldn't stop him from trying.

playerResponses.text: Completing an unfinished delivery. That's a worthy memorial.
I'm sorry about your brother. But retracing his steps feels dangerous.
